tool.name: Page dewarp
tool.description: flatten and threshold warped document images
tool.command: mkdir -p inputs results && #import re #if str($mode.processing) == "batch" #set $images = $mode.input_images #else #set $images = [$mode.input_image] #end if #for $img in $images: #set $safe = re.sub('[^A-Za-z0-9._-]', '_', str($img.element_identifier)) cp '$img' 'inputs/${safe}.${img.ext}' && #end for cd results && page-dewarp --debug-level $debug.debug_level --debug-dest file #if str($mode.processing) == "batch" --batch on #else --batch off #end if --device cpu #if str($optim.method) != "auto" --method '$optim.method' #end if #if str($optim.max_iter): --max-iter $optim.max_iter #end if #if str($optim.max_corrections): --max-corrections $optim.max_corrections #end if #if str($page_detection.x_margin): --x-margin $page_detection.x_margin #end if #if str($page_detection.y_margin): --y-margin $page_detection.y_margin #end if #if str($text_detection.min_text_width): --min-text-width $text_detection.min_text_width #end if #if str($text_detection.min_text_height): --min-text-height $text_detection.min_text_height #end if #if str($text_detection.min_text_aspect): --min-text-aspect $text_detection.min_text_aspect #end if #if str($text_detection.max_text_thickness): --max-text-thickness $text_detection.max_text_thickness #end if #if str($text_detection.adaptive_winsz): --adaptive-winsz $text_detection.adaptive_winsz #end if #if str($span_edge.min_span_width): --min-span-width $span_edge.min_span_width #end if #if str($span_edge.span_spacing): --span-spacing $span_edge.span_spacing #end if #if str($span_edge.max_edge_overlap): --max-edge-overlap $span_edge.max_edge_overlap #end if #if str($span_edge.max_edge_length): --max-edge-length $span_edge.max_edge_length #end if #if str($span_edge.max_edge_angle): --max-edge-angle $span_edge.max_edge_angle #end if #if str($span_edge.edge_angle_cost): --edge-angle-cost $span_edge.edge_angle_cost #end if #if str($camera.focal_length): --focal-length $camera.focal_length #end if #if str($camera.shear_cost): --shear-cost $camera.shear_cost #end if #if str($output_options.output_zoom): --output-zoom $output_options.output_zoom #end if #if str($output_options.output_dpi): --output-dpi $output_options.output_dpi #end if $output_options.no_binary ../inputs/* #if str($mode.processing) == "single" ## In single mode the result is exposed as a plain dataset, so ## rename the lone *_thresh.png to a fixed name from_work_dir ## can pick up. && mv *_thresh.png dewarped_output.png #end if

param.name: processing
param.label: Processing mode
param.help: Batch dewarps several images in a single page-dewarp invocation, amortising startup cost - recommended when you already have a collection or a handful of images. Per-image dewarps a single dataset; map the tool over a collection if you want one job per image (gives per-image error isolation and cluster parallelism).
param.option: Batch - many images in one job (collection-friendly)

param.name: input_images
param.label: Input images
param.help: Select one or more datasets, or a list collection. All selected images are dewarped in one job and returned as a list collection.

param.name: input_image
param.label: Input image
param.help: A single document page image. To run on many images at once, switch to batch mode or map this tool over a collection.

param.label: Output zoom factor
param.help: Zoom of output relative to original image. 2.0 roughly doubles the dimensions.

param.label: Output PNG DPI
param.help: Stated DPI metadata of the output PNG (does not affect appearance).

param.label: Disable binary thresholding
param.help: If enabled, output the remapped grayscale image without adaptive thresholding.

param.label: Optimisation method
param.help: auto picks L-BFGS-B if JAX is available, otherwise Powell.

param.label: Maximum iterations
param.help: Maximum optimiser iterations. Set to 1 for a fast draft preview.

param.label: L-BFGS-B max corrections
param.help: Maximum corrections used to approximate the inverse Hessian (L-BFGS-B only).

param.label: Horizontal margin (px)
param.help: Pixels to ignore near the left and right page edge. Set to 0 if text reaches the edges.

param.label: Vertical margin (px)
param.help: Pixels to ignore near the top and bottom page edge.

param.label: Minimum text contour width (reduced px)
param.help: Contours narrower than this are filtered out.

param.label: Minimum text contour height (reduced px)
param.help: Contours shorter than this are filtered out.

param.label: Minimum text aspect ratio (w/h)
param.help: Filter out text contours below this width/height ratio.

param.label: Maximum text contour thickness (reduced px)
param.help: Contours thicker than this are filtered out.

param.label: Adaptive threshold window size
param.help: Window size for adaptive thresholding. Must be an odd number.

param.label: Maximum edge overlap (reduced px)
param.help: Maximum horizontal overlap of contours within a span.

param.label: Maximum edge length (reduced px)
param.help: Maximum length of edges connecting contours.

param.label: Edge angle cost
param.help: Tradeoff between edge angle and edge length.

param.label: Shear cost
param.help: Penalty against camera tilt. Increase if output appears sheared.

param.label: Debug level
param.help: Higher values save more intermediate images. The debug images are emitted as a separate output collection.

tool.help:
**page-dewarp**
page-dewarp flattens warped photographs of document pages and produces a clean, thresholded image that is much easier to read and to feed into OCR pipelines. It detects text spans on the page, fits a cubic sheet model that approximates the curvature of the paper, and then remaps the original image to a flat plane.
-----
**Inputs and processing modes**
The tool offers two processing modes:
- **Batch (collection-friendly):** select several individual datasets or a list collection of JPEG / PNG / TIFF / BMP images. They are all dewarped in a single page-dewarp invocation - much faster than running one image at a time because page-dewarp's startup cost is paid once instead of N times. The output is a list collection whose element identifiers are the input identifiers (a collection in is a structurally matching collection out). - **Per-image:** select a single image and get back a single dewarped PNG dataset. To dewarp many images this way (one Galaxy job per image, giving per-image error isolation and cluster parallelism), map this tool over a collection.
-----
**Outputs**
- ``dewarped pages`` (batch mode) - a list collection of thresholded PNGs, one per input image, with element identifiers matching the inputs. - ``dewarped page`` (per-image mode) - a single thresholded PNG dataset. - ``debug images`` - only produced when **Debug level** is greater than 0. page-dewarp writes intermediate visualisations of contour detection, span assembly and the optimised cubic sheet model. These are useful when a page fails to dewarp cleanly and you need to understand which step misfired.
-----
**Tips**
- For a fast preview while tuning parameters, set **Maximum iterations** to a small value such as 1. - If the output looks sheared (parallelogram shaped), try raising the **Shear cost** in the *Camera and geometry* section. - If text is cropped from the page edges, set the **Horizontal margin** and **Vertical margin** to 0. - The **Adaptive threshold window size** must be odd. Increase it for pages with mixed text sizes.
